subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements

questions.q: Explain the principles and processes involved in the extraction of metals from their ores. How is the choice of method influenced by the nature of the ore and the metal? (5 marks)
questions.answer: The extraction of metals from their ores depends on the nature of the ore and the metal. Methods are chosen based on the reactivity of the metal, the type of ore, and the cost involved. The main steps in the extraction process are crushing and grinding, concentration, smelting, and refining. The choice of the method also depends on the reactivity of the metal. For example, metals like sodium and magnesium are extracted by electrolysis, while metals like iron and copper are extracted by reduction using carbon.
marking_scheme: Explain the main steps in the extraction process (1 mark)
Discuss the influence of the reactivity of the metal on the choice of method (1 mark)
Describe the role of the type of ore in the choice of method (1 mark)
Mention the cost factor in the choice of method (1 mark)
Provide examples to support the discussion (1 mark)

questions.q: What are the main sources of metals? Explain how different types of ores can be classified based on their chemical properties. (5 marks)
questions.answer: The main sources of metals are ores. Ores can be classified based on their chemical properties into native ores, carbonates, silicates, and oxides. Native ores are made up of metals in their elemental form, such as gold and platinum. Carbonates are found in the form of carbonates of metals like lead and zinc. Silicates are found in the form of silicates of metals like aluminum and silicon. Oxides are found in the form of oxides of metals like iron and titanium.
marking_scheme: Identify the main sources of metals (1 mark)
Explain the classification of ores based on their chemical properties (2 marks)
Describe the characteristics of native ores (1 mark)
Describe the characteristics of carbonates (1 mark)
Describe the characteristics of silicates and oxides (1 mark)

questions.q: Describe the process of refining zinc using the Mond's process. (5 marks)
questions.answer: The process of refining zinc using Mond's process involves the conversion of impure zinc into pure zinc metal. The steps involved are: impure zinc is heated in steam to produce zinc oxide, which is then heated with carbon monoxide to produce zinc vapor. The zinc vapor is then passed through a series of hot tubes, where it is purified. The purified zinc vapor is then condensed and collected in a receiver.
marking_scheme: Describe the conversion of impure zinc into zinc oxide (1 mark)
Explain the reaction with carbon monoxide to produce zinc vapor (1 mark)
Describe the purification of zinc vapor (1 mark)
Describe the condensation and collection of purified zinc (1 mark)
Mention the importance of Mond's process in the production of pure zinc (1 mark)

questions.q: Calculate the mass of copper obtained from 100 g of copper ore containing 80% copper. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Calculation: Mass of copper ore = 100 g Mass of copper = 80% of 100 g = 80 g Mass of impurities = 20 g Mass of copper in 80 g of ore = 80 g Mass of copper in 1 g of ore = 80/80 = 1 g Mass of copper in 100 g of ore = 1 * 100 = 100 g
marking_scheme: Calculate the mass of copper in 100 g of ore (1 mark)
Mention the mass of impurities in the ore (1 mark)
Calculate the mass of copper in 1 g of ore (1 mark)
Calculate the mass of copper in 100 g of ore (1 mark)
Mention the implications of the result (1 mark)

questions.q: A sample of zinc ore contains 25% of zinc. If 100 g of ore is heated in the presence of carbon, 80% of the zinc is reduced to the pure metal. Calculate the mass of pure zinc obtained. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Calculation: Mass of zinc in 100 g of ore = 25% of 100 g = 25 g Mass of zinc reduced to pure metal = 80% of 25 g = 20 g Mass of impurities in 20 g of zinc = 20% of 20 g = 4 g Mass of pure zinc obtained = 20 g - 4 g = 16 g
marking_scheme: Calculate the mass of zinc in 100 g of ore (1 mark)
Calculate the mass of zinc reduced to pure metal (1 mark)
Calculate the mass of impurities in 20 g of zinc (1 mark)
Calculate the mass of pure zinc obtained (1 mark)
Mention the implications of the result (1 mark)

questions.q: Calculate the mass of iron obtained from 200 g of iron ore containing 90% iron, which is reduced to pure iron by heating it in the presence of carbon. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Calculation: Mass of iron in 200 g of ore = 90% of 200 g = 180 g Mass of impurities in 180 g of iron = 10% of 180 g = 18 g Mass of pure iron obtained = 180 g - 18 g = 162 g
marking_scheme: Calculate the mass of iron in 200 g of ore (1 mark)
Calculate the mass of impurities in 180 g of iron (1 mark)
Calculate the mass of pure iron obtained (1 mark)
Mention the implications of the result (1 mark)
Mention the importance of the reduction process (1 mark)
